title: bugsweep run-summary
description: Deterministic, script-emitted reduction of a bugsweep run's ledger.jsonl + recon.json. This is the machine-readable contract a headless scheduler (nightshift) can branch on without parsing model prose. Versioning rule: schema_version bumps ONLY on a breaking change (a required field removed/renamed, or an existing field's meaning/type changed) — adding a new OPTIONAL field, such as root_cause_clusters/follow_up/flaky (bugsweep-xdw), is backward compatible and does NOT bump schema_version.

degraded.description: True only when python3 was unavailable and the reduction fell back to grep-able ledger lines with empty findings.

root_cause_clusters.description: OPTIONAL (bugsweep-xdw). Confirmed/fixed findings sharing a category (or category::variant) cluster together; a singleton (size 1) is not a cluster and is excluded. Ordered size descending, then cluster name ascending.

follow_up.description: OPTIONAL (bugsweep-xdw). The 'where to look next' handoff, derived from recon.json's uncovered batches, prior-coverage.json's high-risk/stale files, and this run's quarantined findings. Ordered uncovered_batch -> high_risk_file -> stale_file -> quarantined, capped at FOLLOW_UP_CAP entries (see bench/scorer/run_summary.py).

flaky.description: OPTIONAL (bugsweep-xdw). One entry per flaky_test ledger event emitted by the test-runner instrumentation; empty when no such events exist.

near_misses.description: OPTIONAL (bugsweep-dxh, --recall mode). Referee near-miss verdicts (confidence 50-67 — genuinely plausible but not >67% CONFIRMED) surfaced for human review. Populated ONLY when reduce_run's 'recall' parameter is true (see bench/scorer/run_summary.py); empty otherwise, even if near_miss ledger events exist. This field is reporting-only: near-misses are NEVER eligible for auto-fix and never appear in fixed/quarantined/confirmed_unfixed/findings, regardless of recall.
